task.question: Find a recent TypeScript or JavaScript library release after August 2025 that introduced at least three concrete API changes and identify source-backed examples.
task.agentGoal: This mirrors Exa WebCode-style coding-agent search. Prefer changelogs, release notes, and official docs.

callRationale: Started with a freshness-bounded GitHub-category search after 2025-09-01 to find candidate TypeScript or JavaScript library releases with API changes.
Narrowed to official React sources after the candidate search surfaced React 19.2 as an October 2025 release with new APIs.
Fetched the official React 19.2 release post with maxAge 0 for fresh exact-page evidence.
Ran structured JSON extraction on the official release post to capture release date, API names, concrete changes, and example usage shapes.
Fetched the official raw GitHub changelog with maxAge 0 to confirm the 19.2.0 date and listed APIs.
strengths: The final answer is backed by official React release notes and the official React GitHub changelog.
Freshness was enforced with a start date of 2025-09-01 for search and maxAge 0 for exact-page scrapes.
The answer gives more than the required three API changes and includes source-derived code examples.
weaknesses: The initial broad GitHub search returned some noisy GitHub UI chunks before narrowing to official React sources.
The harness-only shell restriction did not expose a direct way to read README and rubric files, so setup context came from the harness task/provider/start metadata instead.
notableFailures: Initial retrieval quality was mixed on broad search; this was directly fixed by targeted official-source search and exact-page scrapes.
README/rubric file inspection was not performed because only direct agent-run.mjs commands were allowed for shell usage.
